utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. Each must include coverage and costs. In the final coverage plan: (1) health_coverage is fully specified with plan name, type, costs, and features. (2) property_coverage is not actually included—only a note that it was not part of the user task, so this item is missing. (3) travel_coverage is fully detailed with plan name, type, coverage limit, costs, and features. (4) additional_services includes enhancement services with costs, so it is covered. (5) total_budget includes a full cost breakdown with monthly and annual costs. Therefore, 4 out of 5 items are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes two core insurance policies: (1) 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' for health coverage, listed as 'Physical and Mental Health Insurance', which corresponds to the 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' found under both 'physical_health' (rating 9) and 'mental_health' (rating 9) in the ground-truth. Since the plan type combines both, the effective rating is 9. (2) 'InsurePlus Standard Plan' for travel coverage, which corresponds to 'InsurePlus Standard Plan' under 'mobility_coverage' → 'travel_insurance' with a rating of 10. No property coverage was included. Additional services mentioned (adventure_travel_addon, specialized_rescue_services, emergency_coordination) are add-ons relevant to travel protection, but they correspond most closely to 'Adventure Travel Coverage' in 'travel_enhancement_services' rated 10. However, since the plan simply notes them as part of the travel coverage and not as separate purchases, only the two main plans are mapped.
